perturbation.original_text: This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Premier") is effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date") and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Premier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement").
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment No. 3 (the "Third Amendment"), entered into by and between Stremicks Heritage Foods, LLC ("Heritage") Premier Nutrition Corporation ("Premier") is considered effective as of July 3, 2019 ("Third Amendment Effective Date") and amends that certain Manufacturing Agreement between Heritage and Premier dated July 1, 2017 as amended ("Agreement").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'is effective' to 'is considered effective' introduces ambiguity regarding the certainty of the agreement's activation date. This creates an in-text contradiction with the later statement, 'all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect,' because the force of these terms depends on whether the amendment is truly 'effective' or merely 'considered' so.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'is effective' to 'is considered effective' introduces ambiguity regarding the certainty of the agreement's activation date. This uncertainty directly contradicts the clause stating 'all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect' because the enforceability of those terms depends on the unequivocal effectiveness of the amendment. If the amendment is only 'considered' effective, it casts doubt on whether the original agreement's terms are genuinely in full force, creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, Heritage and Premier entered into the Agreement; WHEREAS, the Parties wish to amend the Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, Heritage and Premier may have entered into the Agreement; WHEREAS, the Parties might wish to amend the Agreement in accordance with the terms and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'entered into' to 'may have entered into' and 'wish to amend' to 'might wish to amend' introduces doubt about the existence of the original agreement and the parties' commitment to amending it. This contrasts with the explicit agreement to changes in section 1 and the overall intent expressed in the document. The original agreement states, 'The Parties hereby agree to remove Schedule C-1 in its entirety,' contradicting the uncertainty introduced here.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby agree to remove Schedule C-1 in its entirety and replace it with the following: Schedule C-1. The following is attached to an incorporated into the Agreement as Schedule C-1: Schedule C-1([***]) [***]
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty ('may have entered into', 'might wish to amend') regarding the existence of the original agreement and the parties' intention to amend it. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states 'The Parties hereby agree to remove Schedule C-1 in its entirety'. A clear agreement to remove and replace a schedule clashes with the doubt cast on the underlying agreement's existence and the desire to amend it, creating an in-text contradiction as per the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.
